_description: Hard and soft contraindication rules for sleep study test selection. Used in M2C (Contraindications & Comorbidity) to determine which tests are blocked, which are recommended against, and which comorbidities affect test routing. Key design principle: O2 is the ONLY hard HST stop. All other comorbidities generate recommendations, not blocks.
_source: Visual Tree v0.19.0 M2C, Audit Report v0.19.0, Developer Handoff v0.19.0 Key Design Decisions

hard_stops._description: Conditions that completely prevent testing. STOP flags -- cannot proceed.

homebound_with_o2.condition: Patient is homebound AND on home oxygen
homebound_with_o2.description: Patient cannot travel to lab AND cannot use HST with O2. No testing option available.
homebound_with_o2.human_action: Document reason. May need NIV (Non-Invasive Ventilation) referral. Fax UTS if applicable.

age_under_2.condition: Patient age < 2 at anticipated service date
age_under_2.description: Cannot perform sleep studies on patients under age 2.
age_under_2.human_action: Notify referring provider. Document and return referral.

hst_hard_block._description: The ONLY clinical condition that blocks HST specifically (besides age < 18). O2 dependency means patient cannot remove supplemental oxygen for home testing.

home_oxygen.condition: Patient uses home supplemental oxygen (O2 dependent)
home_oxygen.description: HST cannot be performed while on supplemental O2. In-lab only.
home_oxygen.human_action: Route to in-lab testing. HST physically not possible.

soft_recommendations._description: Comorbidities that generate FLAG_PSG_RECOMMENDED but do NOT remove HST from scope. Physician order still controls. The system recommends PSG but lets HST proceed if ordered.

copd_moderate_severe.reason: Potential central/complex apnea, O2 desaturation monitoring beneficial in-lab

chf.reason: Associated with central sleep apnea and Cheyne-Stokes respiration

opioid_use.reason: Opioids cause central apnea and hypoventilation -- in-lab monitoring preferred

morbid_obesity.reason: Obesity hypoventilation syndrome risk, complex apnea patterns
morbid_obesity.notes: BMI > 30 but < 50 is noted for clinical context but does not generate this flag.

neuromuscular_disease.reason: Central/complex apnea risk, need respiratory monitoring

suspected_central_apnea.condition: Suspected or known central sleep apnea
suspected_central_apnea.reason: HST cannot detect central apneas -- in-lab required for accurate diagnosis

multiple_moderate_comorbidities.condition: 2+ moderate comorbidities from the list above present simultaneously
multiple_moderate_comorbidities.reason: Multiple comorbidities increase complexity -- clinical review recommended
multiple_moderate_comorbidities.human_action: Flag for clinical review. Consider PSG over HST.

pediatric_restrictions._description: Age-based test restrictions. Checked in M1B and enforced in M2C/M4.

age_2_to_5.restriction: Pediatric codes only (95782/95783). HST not allowed.
age_2_to_5.reason: HST not validated for pediatric patients under 6.

age_6_to_17.restriction: Adult PSG codes (95810/95811). HST not allowed under 18.
age_6_to_17.reason: HST not validated for pediatric patients.

age_18_plus.restriction: No pediatric restrictions. Full adult scope available.

titration_prerequisites._description: Titration (95811/95783) requires a prior positive diagnostic study. Checked in M2B.

path_a_pressure.description: Patient already on PAP, needs pressure adjustment.
path_a_pressure.requirement: Most recent titration results showing inadequate pressure.

path_b_diagnosis.description: Patient has prior positive diagnostic, needs first titration.

design_principles.scope_never_expands: The physician's order defines scope. If order says 'diagnostic only', system never adds titration -- even if clinically indicated. It flags the recommendation but doesn't override the physician.
design_principles.o2_is_only_hard_hst_stop: Every other comorbidity (COPD, CHF, opioids, BMI > 50) generates FLAG_PSG_RECOMMENDED but HST remains available. Only home oxygen physically prevents HST.
design_principles.recommendations_not_blocks: Soft recommendations inform the clinical picture but do not override physician judgment or remove options from scope.
